import { pageMeta } from "@/lib/og";
import GatewayLogos from "@/components/GatewayLogos";
import ProviderKeyTable from "@/components/ProviderKeyTable";

export const metadata = pageMeta("Models & Credentials", "Declare your model in agent YAML and let Omnigent manage the credentials that authenticate to each provider.", {
  eyebrow: "Build",
  path: "/docs/build/models",
});

# Models & Credentials

Every harness needs a model to run and credentials to reach it. You declare the model in
your agent YAML, and Omnigent manages the credentials that authenticate to each provider.

## Choose a model

Declare the model in your agent YAML:

```
executor:
  harness: claude-sdk
  model: claude-sonnet-4-6
```

Override it at runtime without editing the file:

```
omni run agent.yaml --model claude-sonnet-4-6
```

### Switch models mid-session

Use the `/model` command inside any running session to switch models on the fly.
The agent keeps its conversation history and tool state – only the underlying model changes.

## Credentials

Omnigent needs credentials to talk to a model provider.

```
omni setup
```

The setup wizard walks you through adding a credential, setting a default, or removing one.
It also auto-detects credentials already on your machine (API keys in environment variables,
logged-in CLIs, and local servers) so you can confirm them with one tap.

## Credential types

| Type | What it is |
| --- | --- |
| **API key** | A first-party provider key (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini/Antigravity, OpenRouter, and more) |
| **Subscription** | A Claude Pro/Max or ChatGPT Plus/Pro plan, via the official `claude` / `codex` CLIs |
| **Gateway** | Bring your own proxy or local model. Just paste a base URL and key. |
| **Databricks** | A Databricks workspace profile, routing through the [Foundation Model API](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/machine-learning/foundation-model-apis/) |
| **Native provider login** | Runtime-owned auth, such as Cursor, Kimi Code, Qwen Code, Goose, OpenCode, Hermes, Kiro, or GitHub Copilot |

### API key

Get a key from your provider's dashboard and add it via `omni setup`.
Supported providers:

<ProviderKeyTable />

### Subscription

If you have a **Claude Pro/Max** or **ChatGPT Plus/Pro** plan,
Omnigent can use your existing CLI login. No API key needed. Just make sure you're
logged in:

```
claude auth login    # for Claude Pro/Max
codex login          # for ChatGPT
```

`omni setup` auto-detects an active CLI login and offers it as a one-tap option.

Some harnesses authenticate with their own provider rather than through an
OpenAI- or Anthropic-compatible endpoint:

| Harness | Authentication |
| --- | --- |
| `cursor` / `cursor-native` | Cursor API key or `cursor-agent login`; use Cursor model IDs such as `auto` or `gpt-5`. |
| `antigravity` / `antigravity-native` | Gemini or Antigravity API key, or Vertex AI credentials. |
| `copilot` | GitHub token with Copilot access. |
| `kimi` / `kimi-native` | `kimi login`, or Kimi gateway/provider settings. |
| `qwen` / `qwen-native` | Qwen Code CLI auth. |
| `goose` / `goose-native` | Goose provider configuration. |
| `opencode-native` | OpenCode auth and provider configuration. |
| `hermes` / `hermes-native` | Hermes CLI auth and configuration. |
| `kiro-native` | Kiro CLI auth. |

Those harnesses can still run inside Omnigent sessions, but their model IDs and
auth setup come from the underlying runtime.

### Gateway

Omnigent works with any **OpenAI-compatible** or **Anthropic-compatible** gateway:

<GatewayLogos />

`omni setup` asks for a **base URL** and a **key**. The base URL differs depending on the agent:

| Provider | For | Base URL | Key |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **OpenRouter** | Claude Code | `https://openrouter.ai/api` | your OpenRouter key (`sk-or-...`) |
| **OpenRouter** | Codex / OpenAI agents | `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` | your OpenRouter key (`sk-or-...`) |
| **Ollama** (local) | Codex / OpenAI agents | `http://localhost:11434/v1` | any value (Ollama ignores it) |

<div className="note">

**Note:** For Claude Code, point at OpenRouter's Anthropic-compatible
endpoint (`.../api`, **not** `.../api/v1`); for Codex
and the OpenAI-agents harness, use the OpenAI-compatible `.../api/v1`.

</div>

### Databricks

Route models through your Databricks workspace using the
[Foundation Model API](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/machine-learning/foundation-model-apis/).
Omnigent resolves credentials from your `~/.databrickscfg` profile.

```
executor:
  harness: claude-sdk
  model: databricks-claude-sonnet-4-6
  auth:
    type: databricks
    profile: <your-profile>
```

Model names are prefixed with `databricks-` so Omnigent routes them through your
workspace automatically. See the
[Databricks Foundation Model API docs](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/machine-learning/foundation-model-apis/supported-models)
for the full list of available models.

### Bedrock

AWS Bedrock is supported as a model provider. Configure AWS credentials in your
environment and select Bedrock-backed model IDs from your Omnigent provider
configuration.

### Model routing

Policies can route or block model usage based on the session context. For
example, the built-in cost policies can warn at soft thresholds, require a
downgrade after a hard budget, or steer trivial requests away from expensive
models. See [Builtin Policies](/docs/policies/builtin#cost-control).
